conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 related entry, [mem-id:27] gives the root formula for a quadratic equation in the field of mathematics x = (-b ± √(b² - 4ac)) / 2a. Given a=1, b=-5, c=6. Substituting into the formula, the part under the square root is (-5)² - 4×1×6 = 25 - 24 = 1. So x = (5 ± 1) / 2, calculating the two roots as x1=3, x2=2 respectively.
conversations.content: According to the rules in the field of mathematics, the roots of the equation are x1 = 3, x2 = 2.
